prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, landscape orientation
Primary request: Mutant: Year Zero / The Ark inspired world map for the terrain type continent_archipelago.
Scene/backdrop: one dominant post-apocalyptic continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago of drowned old-world districts, rusting tower islands, reactor islets, mutant settlements, Zone sectors, scavenger ferry routes, dead radiation zones, toxic marsh deltas, ruined harbors, collapsed bridge chains and overgrown industrial coastlands.
Style/medium: high-resolution illustrated survival atlas, grey-green toxic palette, weathered map paper, gritty inked coastlines, hand-painted terrain relief, ruin markers, hazard symbols, scavenged Zone cartography.
Composition/framing: wide landscape map; main continent placed off-center with an irregular island chain sweeping around it in a diagonal arc; islands vary from micro-islets and reef belts to large ruined landmasses; include compass, sector lines and worn border ornaments without readable text.
Geography constraints: islands must be irregularly distributed and diverse in size and shape, with uneven channels, lagoons, wreck fields, flooded suburbs, volcanic or crater islands and broken coastlines; continental shore includes peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, deltas, marshes, eroded cliffs and curved coasts; show deserts, forests, toxic swamps, snowy highlands, ruined city belts and barren dead zones.
Avoid: tropical paradise, uniform island grid, simple round islands, clean fantasy map, modern satellite image, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
